title: Cuteness Overload

jokes.text: You double-dipped! Geez.
jokes.setup: Fry does something disgusting at a social gathering
jokes.punchline: He double-dipped in what's presumably a communal dip
jokes.explanation: Simple character comedy showing Fry's lack of social awareness. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Why, I don't even know half your names! You, boy. What do they call you? Most folks just call me Orange Joe.
jokes.setup: Professor admits he doesn't know his crew's names
jokes.punchline: Reveals there's a crew member literally named 'Orange Joe'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Professor's obliviousness, with a memorable absurd name that sets up future callback potential.

jokes.text: It's July, right? Let's wait three months and go to Oktoberfest!
jokes.setup: Professor suggests a fun outing for the crew
jokes.punchline: His idea of immediate fun is waiting three months
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor logic - completely missing the point of spontaneity. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Bring on the beer! And the scantily clad barmaids! And the more beer!
jokes.setup: Bender's excitement about Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: His priorities: beer, women, more beer - in that order
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender hedonism, well-executed but predictable. The escalation structure works.

jokes.text: Hey! I don't smell Bavarian-style vomit. Where's Oktoberfest?
jokes.setup: Bender expects traditional rowdy Oktoberfest atmosphere
jokes.punchline: He identifies Oktoberfest by the smell of vomit
jokes.explanation: Excellent specificity with 'Bavarian-style vomit' - absurd but logical for Bender. Very quotable.

jokes.text: No, I wouldn't want to ruin my Oktoberfest by becoming intoxicated.
jokes.setup: Upscale patron at Oktoberfest offered another drink
jokes.punchline: He'd rather not get drunk at a beer festival
jokes.explanation: Good irony about missing the point of Oktoberfest. Sets up the theme of over-sophistication.

jokes.text: You can all act like Jersey Shore socialites, but at least Bender will party with me. Right, buddy?
jokes.setup: Fry complains about everyone being too refined, expects Bender's support
jokes.punchline: Calling the sophisticated behavior 'Jersey Shore socialites' - backwards reference
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion - the refined behavior is compared to Jersey Shore. Sets up Bender's betrayal.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I enjoy partying as much as the next fellow, but this is Oktoberfest. Good day, sir!
jokes.setup: Fry expects Bender to support his desire to party hard
jokes.punchline: Even party robot Bender is being stuffy and formal
jokes.explanation: Excellent character reversal - Bender being refined is deeply wrong. The formal 'Good day, sir!' is perfect.

jokes.text: Ach du freakin' Lieber!
jokes.setup: Fry is frustrated with the overly refined Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: He attempts German but substitutes 'freakin'' in the phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's attempt to fit in while maintaining his crude vocabulary.

jokes.text: Sounds like fun on a bun! Shut up! I said it first! Bam! Also, ow.
jokes.setup: Fry makes a pun about the sausage contest
jokes.punchline: Elzar violently claims ownership of the pun and immediately regrets the violence
jokes.explanation: Good Elzar character moment with his signature 'Bam!' and immediate consequence. The 'also, ow' adds nice deflation.

jokes.text: Hey, Elzar, I've just recently started always having dreamt of being a sausage-making champion.
jokes.setup: Fry wants to join the sausage contest
jokes.punchline: His mangled grammar trying to sound like he's always dreamed of this
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry-ism with the tortured grammar trying to sound sophisticated. Very quotable construction.

jokes.text: Gonna grind 'em up into Heavenwurst.
jokes.setup: Chef explains his exotic sausage made from angel wings
jokes.punchline: The portmanteau 'Heavenwurst' combining heaven and bratwurst
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both absurd and logical. Very economical and memorable portmanteau.

jokes.text: So you are using pork! Pork that's been aged for 3,000 years! Bam!
jokes.setup: Fry calls out Elzar for using regular pork despite his criticism
jokes.punchline: Elzar reveals his pork is absurdly aged, maintaining superiority
jokes.explanation: Good escalation preserving Elzar's ego while staying absurd. The 'Bam!' punctuation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Sorry, Grundy. I'll have to kill you later for some other reason.
jokes.setup: Elzar addressing his pig before the contest
jokes.punchline: Casual promise to kill the pig for unrelated reasons later
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. Good economy and character voice, though the darkness is somewhat expected for Futurama.

jokes.text: Man, all the fun has been taken out of this once-noble barf-a-palooza.
jokes.setup: Fry complains about the refined Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: He calls the original Oktoberfest a 'barf-a-palooza' but considers it noble
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay with 'barf-a-palooza' and the ironic 'once-noble' elevates crude behavior. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm as thirsty as a drunk.
jokes.setup: Fry expresses his thirst
jokes.punchline: Comparing his thirst to that of a drunk person (who would be very thirsty)
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on the common phrase structure. Economical and has a weird logic to it.

jokes.text: Fahrvergnugen! Kaiser Permanente! Fruzen Gladje!
jokes.setup: Drunk Fry attempts to speak German
jokes.punchline: He shouts random German-sounding brand names instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy mixing Volkswagen ad slogan, health insurer, and Häagen-Dazs. Shows perfect Fry confusion between German and German-ish marketing.

jokes.text: Ich bin ein never!
jokes.setup: Refined patron refuses to participate in Fry's antics
jokes.punchline: Butchers JFK's famous 'Ich bin ein Berliner' to say he's a 'never'
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference twisted into a refusal. Very economical and works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Okay, that's it. You are a boorish, primitive Neanderthal! We were going out? I mean, no!
jokes.setup: Leela breaks up with Fry, calling him a Neanderthal
jokes.punchline: She accidentally reveals they were dating, then tries to cover it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's attempt to maintain dignity while contradicting herself. The 'Neanderthal' insult becomes plot-relevant.

jokes.text: And the worst part is, I had to have the breakup sex by myself!
jokes.setup: Fry complains about his breakup
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic and pathetic 'breakup sex by myself'
jokes.explanation: Somewhat cheap masturbation joke but elevated by the euphemistic phrasing and Fry's pathetic delivery.

jokes.text: I hate future Oktoberfest! It's supposed to be beer and bratwurst, not breakups and boring thing!
jokes.setup: Fry rants about how Oktoberfest has changed
jokes.punchline: His alliterative complaint ends lamely with 'boring thing'
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with the alliteration attempt that falls apart, showing Fry's limited vocabulary.

jokes.text: where prehistoric man once hunted the majestic Schnufel-Ufagus or woolly mammoth.
jokes.setup: Educational explanation about prehistoric times
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous German-sounding name 'Schnufel-Ufagus' for mammoth
jokes.explanation: Good absurd German-ish name construction, very quotable but not deeply integrated into character or plot.

jokes.text: I didn't know this ship had a mammoth detector. You're drunk, Fry. This is the elephant detector. I just set it to 'big and woolly.'
jokes.setup: Fry comments on the ship's mammoth detection capability
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals it's just an elephant detector with creative settings
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic about future technology. The specificity of 'big and woolly' as detector settings is excellent.

jokes.text: My hair is caught! Bender! Turn it off! Sausage is great, sausage is great. Sausage, sausage, sausage!
jokes.setup: Fry gets caught in the meat grinder
jokes.punchline: His cries for help devolve into mindless sausage chanting
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with absurd brainwashing-like chanting. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Shut up, Judge, I know it. Way to go, Bender! Can I try a piece? You can have yourself a whole big boy.
jokes.setup: Bender's sausage wins, Leela wants to try some
jokes.punchline: Bender generously offers her a 'big boy' portion of what's actually Fry
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Bender's obliviousness and generosity with human meat. Good character voice but the cannibalism is somewhat expected for the show.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! That looks like Fry's hair! Come on, don't make jokes like that while I'm eating.
jokes.setup: Leela discovers hair in her sausage that looks like Fry's
jokes.punchline: Someone dismisses it as a joke, not realizing it's literally true
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony - audience knows the truth while character dismisses it. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's tragedy on a bun.
jokes.setup: The crew mourns Fry's apparent death
jokes.punchline: Professor's pun combining the tragedy with the sausage bun
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun structure playing on 'comedy/tragedy' with perfect economy. Dark but not mean-spirited. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Now, now. We've all been there.
jokes.setup: Leela confesses she ate her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Professor casually suggests this is a common experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating cannibalism as mundane relationship experience. Great Professor character voice.

jokes.text: Like this one 'Fry falls off zoo train.' He thought he recognized one of the monkeys!
jokes.setup: Surgeon describes memories being removed
jokes.punchline: Fry's stupid reason for falling - thought he knew a monkey
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - the kind of dumb thing only he would do. Good character specificity and visual comedy.

jokes.text: 'Fry finds popcorn in his belly button.' 'You eat a sausage made of Fry.' 'Fry gives you a chicken hat.' Oh, that's sweet.
jokes.setup: Surgeon continues listing memories to be severed
jokes.punchline: The traumatic cannibalism memory is sandwiched between gross-cute moments, ending with 'that's sweet'
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of disturbing with mundane/sweet. The callback to the chicken hat works well. Structure creates cognitive dissonance.

jokes.text: Geez, who died?
jokes.setup: Fry returns to find everyone mourning
jokes.punchline: He asks who died, when everyone thinks HE died
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony moment. Functional but predictable given the setup.

jokes.text: What about my feelings? Fry was my best friend, and now he's dead! Nah, I'm just kidding. My pastor helped me through my grief.
jokes.setup: Bender seems to have genuine feelings about Fry's death
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he was faking, then mentions having a pastor
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment with the fake emotion followed by unexpected religious reference. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Sausage is great, sausage is great. Sausage, sausage, sausage. Whew! That was close.
jokes.setup: Fry almost gets brainwashed again by his traumatic experience
jokes.punchline: He snaps out of the sausage chanting
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier trauma but not particularly innovative. Shows ongoing effects of the experience.

jokes.text: Me think so. Anyone dumb enough get stuck in ice probably Neanderthal.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if he's one of the Neanderthals
jokes.punchline: Neanderthal insults Neanderthal intelligence while being one himself
jokes.explanation: Good irony with the self-deprecating Neanderthal. Nice callback to Leela's insult that becomes literally true.

jokes.text: Fifty three tons of hamburgers and French Fragments of not potatoes.
jokes.setup: Describing a delivery to McPluto
jokes.punchline: French fries are called 'French Fragments of not potatoes'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on fast food naming conventions. Very quotable but not deeply integrated into character or plot.

jokes.text: Why you stare at prehistoric pig butt?
jokes.setup: Fry staring at cave painting
jokes.punchline: Blunt caveman description of what he's looking at
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective caveman directness

jokes.text: All Neanderthal sad. It in our nature. Why? It all start 30,000 year ago, in fertile Neander Valley.
jokes.setup: Fry asks why he feels sad
jokes.punchline: Chief explains it's just the nature of being Neanderthal, then launches into exposition
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the backstory while maintaining the broken English character voice. Functional exposition humor.

jokes.text: We suggest interbreed, become one big happy species. But they treat us like second-class hominid.
jokes.setup: Explaining Neanderthal-human relations
jokes.punchline: The clinical term 'second-class hominid' for discrimination
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay applying modern discrimination terminology to prehistoric times. Works as social commentary.

jokes.text: And I do have vague memories of people refusing to breed with me!
jokes.setup: Fry relates to the Neanderthal struggle
jokes.punchline: He connects it to his own romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment connecting prehistoric persecution to his personal romantic failures. Good character integration.

jokes.text: This very interesting. Maybe someone look into it, many years from now.
jokes.setup: Told about hole leading to outside world
jokes.punchline: Chief shows no urgency about their freedom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Neanderthal procrastination/apathy. The understatement works well.

jokes.text: It's our chance to take back the outside world from those stuck-up Homo sapiens with their tools and their pants and such.
jokes.setup: Neanderthal gets excited about escaping
jokes.punchline: Lists 'tools and pants' as evidence of human arrogance
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic treating basic civilization as pretentious. The escalation from tools to pants is perfect.

jokes.text: This is the greatest injustice Germany has ever committed!
jokes.setup: Bender only gets third place in sausage contest
jokes.punchline: He claims it's Germany's greatest injustice (ignoring actual historical atrocities)
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Bender narcissism. The historical ignorance/perspective is brilliantly executed and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Dear God, look at those hairy elephants!
jokes.setup: Zapp sees Neanderthal attack
jokes.punchline: Mistakes mammoths for hairy elephants
jokes.explanation: Fits Zapp's character - technically not wrong but missing the point

jokes.text: With men in them. Then how are they unmanned? The men are just for ballast.
jokes.setup: Zapp describes unmanned drones
jokes.punchline: They have men in them, but only as ballast (weight)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp logic solving a contradiction with more absurdity. Great character voice and the ballast explanation is brilliant.

jokes.text: it's designed to withstand even the weight of a modern-day elephant foot.
jokes.setup: Zapp brags about his drone's durability
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets crushed by mammoth foot (close enough to elephant)
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with immediate ironic reversal. Good character moment for Zapp's overconfidence.

jokes.text: like the mighty 'X' in tic-tac-toe.
jokes.setup: Zapp compares their aerial advantage to being invincible
jokes.punchline: His analogy is to tic-tac-toe, a children's game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp analogy - completely inappropriate scale and wrong game theory. Great character voice and very quotable.

jokes.text: We want our land back! Also, to be treated as equals! And a little mating wouldn't hurt!
jokes.setup: Neanderthals state their demands
jokes.punchline: Escalates from political to personal - they just want to get laid
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from noble political demands to basic biological needs. Works as callback to earlier themes.

jokes.text: Orange? Purple? Do I know you? Have we met?
jokes.setup: Fry encounters Orange Joe during the battle
jokes.punchline: Almost remembers him but can't quite place him
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Fry's memory issues. The color confusion adds character consistency.

jokes.text: Guys, guys! Chef Fritz accidentally got thrown down an icy slope! I'm the sausage champion! No further questions!
jokes.setup: Bender announces he won the sausage contest
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions eliminating the competition 'accidentally', then shuts down inquiry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment with obvious guilt followed by immediate deflection. The 'no further questions' is excellent character voice.

jokes.text: No, Leela. Just this once, I'm going to let you embarrass me.
jokes.setup: Leela invites Fry to dance the chicken dance
jokes.punchline: Fry frames not participating as doing Leela a favor
jokes.explanation: Nice character growth moment with Fry being selfless. Good callback to the embarrassment theme while inverting it.

tmdb_overview: Amy sets out to collect the cutest toys in the universe – but they are not as harmless as they appear.
